title: Popover
subtitle: Interactive floating content component with support for text, templates and dynamic components. Unlike Tooltip, the popover can contain interactive elements such as buttons, forms or lists.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Simple popover with text content. The popover is displayed on click (default behavior).

colors.title: Colors and variants
colors.description: Supports all NUI colors and variants (Solid, Outline, Ghost) to customize the popover appearance.
colors.note: Combine color and variant to adapt the popover style to your design.

positions.title: Positions
positions.description: Control the preferred popover position: top, bottom, left or right. The popover repositions automatically if there is not enough space.

events.title: Activation events
events.description: Define how the popover is activated: click (default), hover, focus or manual. Manual mode allows complete programmatic control.
events.note: When using nuiPopoverEvent we have three modes: 'click' (show/hide on click), 'hover' (show on mouse over and hide on mouse out) and 'focus' (show on focus and hide on blur).

template.title: Custom template
template.description: Use Angular templates for rich, interactive content. The template context provides a close function to close the popover.
template.note: The #popoverTemplate template receives a context with the close() function that you can use to close the popover from inside the template.

component.title: Dynamic component
component.description: Render full Angular components inside the popover. Ideal for forms, lists or complex content.

delays.title: Show/hide delays
delays.description: Configure delays in milliseconds before showing or hiding the popover. Useful for improving the user experience in hover mode.

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: Customize the popover appearance: no arrow, custom width, own CSS classes or larger separation offset.
customization.note: You can use nuiPopoverShowArrow to hide/show the arrow, nuiPopoverMaxWidth to define a maximum width, nuiPopoverMinWidth to define a minimum width, nuiPopoverClass to add custom CSS classes, and nuiPopoverOffset to adjust the distance between the popover and the target element.

closeOptions.title: Close options
closeOptions.description: Control how the popover can be closed: outside click, Escape key, or allow multiple popovers open simultaneously.
closeOptions.note: Use nuiPopoverCloseOnClickOutside to close on outside click, nuiPopoverCloseOnEscape to close with Escape, and nuiPopoverAllowMultiple to allow multiple popovers open at the same time.

backdrop.title: Backdrop
backdrop.description: Adds a dark background behind the popover to create a modal-like experience. You can control whether clicking on the backdrop closes the popover.
backdrop.note: Use nuiPopoverBackdrop to enable/disable the backdrop and nuiBackdropClose to control whether clicking the backdrop closes the popover.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Dynamically control whether the popover is enabled or disabled.
disabled.note: When nuiPopoverDisabled is true, the popover will not show or respond to events.

config.title: PopoverConfig
config.description: Interface for global popover configuration via provideNUI({ popover: {...} })

context.title: PopoverContext
context.description: Interface for the context available in popover templates via let-close="close" and let-data="data"

tokens.title: Injection tokens
tokens.description: Tokens for injecting data and functions into dynamic popover components

spacing.title: Spacing and dimensions
spacing.description: CSS variables to control the size, padding and spacing of the popover

typography.title: Typography
typography.description: CSS variables to control the font of the popover content

borders.title: Borders
borders.description: CSS variables to control the borders and radius of the popover

shadow.title: Shadow
shadow.description: CSS variable to control the popover shadow

zindex.title: Z-index
zindex.description: CSS variable to control the stacking order of the popover

animation.title: Animation
animation.description: CSS variables to control popover transitions and animations

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The Popover implements the ARIA non-modal dialog pattern. The trigger announces whether the popover is open and indicates the relationship with its content.

ariaExpanded.description: The trigger carries aria-expanded reflecting whether the popover is visible (true) or not (false).

ariaControls.description: aria-controls on the trigger points to the id of the popover panel.

ariaHaspopup.description: aria-haspopup="dialog" indicates that activating the trigger opens an interactive content panel.

popoverRole.description: The popover panel has role="dialog" and aria-label derived from its title or content.

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The popover and its trigger must have accessible names that clearly communicate their purpose.
naming.note: Always provide an aria-label or a visible title in the popover so screen readers can correctly identify it.

trigger.description: The trigger must have its own accessible name (visible text, aria-label…) that describes the action of opening the popover.

panel.description: The popover panel gets its accessible name from the visible title or an explicit aria-label.

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: The Popover follows the WAI-ARIA non-modal dialog keyboard pattern.

enterSpace.description: Opens or closes the popover when the trigger has focus.

escape.description: Closes the popover and returns focus to the trigger.

tab.description: Cycles focus between interactive elements inside the open panel.
